name: Everyone's Favorite Coffee Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Fresh raspberries (or frozen unsweetened whole raspberries)
1 cup Fresh blueberries, picked over, rinsed, and patted dry (or frozen unsweetened whole blueberries)
5 tablespoons granulated sugar plus 1/2 cup granulated sugar, divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F and position a rack in the center. Coat a 10-inch pie plate or an 8- or 9-inch square pan with cooking spray.
2. In the prepared pan, combine the fruit, 5 tablespoons of sugar, and cinnamon. Toss lightly and set aside.
3. In a large bowl, whisk together egg whites, remaining sugar, oil, applesauce, juice, and extract. Place a strainer over the bowl and add flour, baking powder, salt. Stir until combined with the wet ingredients; do not overbeat.
4. If using toasted wheat germ, combine it with butter (or margarine) in the small bowl. Mix until crumbly; add oil and juice if needed to form crumbs.
5. Pour the batter into the pan; some fruit may peek through. Sprinkle the crumb topping evenly over the cake.
6. Bake for 35-40 minutes when using fresh fruit, or 50 minutes with frozen fruit, until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean and the top is golden brown and springy to the touch. Cool slightly on a wire rack before serving warm.
Cake remains best immediately after baking but can be stored covered at room temperature for up to two days. Warm before serving. For less dense crumb, omit applesauce and replace with 1/3 cup oil. Choose from available berries or fruits (blueberries with cranberries or peach slices). If margarine is used in the topping, no dairy is present, making it suitable for lactose-intolerant diets.
